tweet.body: TRUMP: WILL HAVE EXTENSIVE DISCUSSION WITH CHINA'S PRESIDENT XI REGARDING IRAN WAR

related_markets.title: Participation in Iran Negotiations
related_markets.question: Will China announce participation in Iran negotiations by May 22?
related_markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Xi Jinping, the Chinese government, or any authorized representative of the Chinese government announces that China will participate in or facilitate negotiations between the United States and Iran between market creation and May 22, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying announcement must explicitly indicate that the Chinese government will participate in or facilitate negotiations between the United States and Iran, including by mediating negotiations, hosting a diplomatic meeting, transmitting proposals or messages between the parties, or negotiating with Iran on behalf of the United States.

Only definitive announcements will qualify. Suggestions, negotiations, expressions of openness, general support for diplomacy, or other non-definitive statements will not qualify.

Any qualifying announcement within this market’s time frame will count, regardless of whether or when the negotiations take place.

This market’s primary resolution source will be official information from the Chinese government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
related_markets.impact_reason: Discussion on Iran negotiations indicates potential announcement (1 hop)
related_markets.event_title: Trump-Xi Summit: What will China announce by May 22?

telegram.text:
📝Referendum Against Keir Starmer📝
And he lost it

Local and regional elections took place in the United Kingdom. At stake were more than 5,000 seats in 136 councils, all London boroughs, six mayors in England plus the parliaments of Scotland and Wales.

🔻Voting results:
▪️The Reform Party achieved a breakthrough and won approximately 1,450 seats — a colossal gain compared to the previous cycle, when the faction had only a handful of deputies. Labour finished the elections with 1,068 seats, losing around 1,500 seats compared to the previous elections.

▪️Conservatives retained 801 seats but lost approximately 560 seats. Liberal Democrats won 834 seats and gained around 150 seats, Greens increased their representation by approximately 400 seats, and independents and local groups took around 212 seats, increasing their share by roughly thirty seats.

▪️Separately on the same day, parliamentary elections took place in Scotland. Following the vote, the Scottish National Party remains the largest force but without an absolute majority, while parties advocating a tougher agenda on autonomy and socio-economic policy are gaining strength.

▪️Similar elections were held for the Welsh parliament, where a regional legislative assembly is also being formed for a new term. The results there are less dramatic than in England and Scotland: local autonomist parties and the Labour camp as a whole maintain their positions, but the overall trend is the same — declining trust in traditional parties and growing support for more radical and protest movements.

In terms of a hypothetical national result, the Reform Party takes first place — approximately 27% of votes. Conservatives receive approximately 20%, Labour — 15–16%, Liberal Democrats and Greens — roughly 14% each.

📌The result for the first time elevates the Reform Party to the status of the largest force at the local level and simultaneously records Labour's largest decline in seats among all major parties.

Prime Minister Keir Starmer acknowledged the results as "tough", took responsibility for the defeat, but refused to resign, saying he will not "plunge the country into chaos" and such days only "strengthen his resolve".

❗️For British politics, this is a "Farage moment". His party is picking up protest voters both from disappointed Labour working-class areas and from traditionally conservative periphery.

If the trend consolidates, traditional parties will face an even harder time in the next general elections, although a decisive success for the Reform Party is unlikely given the peculiarities of the British electoral system, which limits the direct influence of minor parties.

@rybar

related_markets.title: May 15
related_markets.question: Starmer out by May 15, 2026?
related_markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Keir Starmer ceases to be the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom for any period of time between market creation and May 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Keir Starmer's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be the government of the UK, however a consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.
related_markets.impact_reason: Labour's decline in seats suggests increased risk of Starmer's removal (1 hop).

telegram.text: Successes in the Forests. The situation in the Slavyansk direction has remained unchanged for quite some time. Before the ceasefire, Russian forces were advancing along the Krivaya Luka-Kaleniki line, advancing toward the Piskunovka-Rai-Aleksandrovka line. And, judging by the emerging footage, the attacks continue. The main tactical objective is several forested areas between these villages, which will allow for further escalation of the assault on both settlements. To the southeast, near Nikiforovka, Russian forces have captured several more forested areas. The Ukrainian Armed Forces previously maintained a pocket of presence there, but this has now been significantly reduced. Against this backdrop, the enemy is attempting to strengthen its own positions in Rai-Aleksandrovka, while fierce fighting between small groups continues to the south along the Dibrova-Minkivka line. Both the Ukrainian Armed Forces and the Russian Armed Forces are operating in small groups there. The main confrontation is taking place east of the Seversky Donets-Donbas Canal. This barrier is already a huge, dry moat with concrete banks, the possession of which will allow the Russian Armed Forces to strengthen their positions and begin building bridges for further attacks toward Kramatorsk. The enemy is trying to delay this moment as long as possible, buying time to fortify the cities. @rybar

related_markets.title: Sloviansk
related_markets.question: Will Russia enter Sloviansk by December 31, 2026?
related_markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Sloviansk (48.853751256046095° N, 37.60508085394494° E; https://maps.app.goo.gl/Kx9MMw32sFDPUt8f9) by December 31, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET.

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the city or settlement is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.
related_markets.impact_reason: Clear military advances toward Sloviansk indicate high probability of entry.
related_markets.event_title: Which cities will Russia enter by December 31?

related_markets.title: December 31
related_markets.question: NATO x Russia military clash by December 31, 2026?
related_markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of a NATO country and Russia between January 2, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between NATO and Russian military forces. Non-violent actions, such as airspace violations, firing of warning shots (such as the June, 2021 Black Sea Confrontations between Russian forces and HMS Defender), or cyberattacks will not qualify.

Interception of missiles or other one-way attack or loitering munitions (e.g. Shahed drones) which are targeting a 3rd party other than the listed countries or their respective forces will not alone qualify. Shooting down UAVs which are not munitions (e.g. MQ-9, Orlan 10, Orion, Bayraktar TB2, etc.) will qualify.

Intentional physical collisions, including aerial interceptions and naval ramming without the direct use of weaponry, such as the 2023 Black Sea incident—where a Russian Su-27 damaged a U.S. MQ-9 Reaper drone's propeller, leading to its crash— will not qualify regardless of damage.

Military contractors will qualify only if confirmed to be operating under the direct command or coordination of the respective state’s armed forces (e.g. the Battle of Khasham would not qualify).

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.
related_markets.impact_reason: Ongoing military actions suggest increased likelihood of clash by December.
related_markets.event_title: NATO x Russia military clash by...?

telegram.text: US Senate confirms Kevin Warsh to be Federal Reserve Governor.|FJ

related_markets.title: Thom Tillis
related_markets.question: Will Thom Tillis vote to confirm Kevin Warsh as Chair of the Federal Reserve?
related_markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Senator votes “Yea” on the first final U.S. Senate confirmation vote on the nomination of Kevin Warsh to be Chair of the Federal Reserve. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The vote refers to the first final confirmation vote on the nomination in the full chamber, not including committee votes or procedural motions.

If the nomination passes unanimously or without individual voting (e.g. a voice vote), this market will resolve to “Yes”. If the nomination is rejected unanimously or without individual voting, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no qualifying vote is held by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”. If a formally submitted nomination is withdrawn or returned to the President before reaching a final confirmation vote, this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

The resolution sources will be official Senate voting records and a consensus of credible reporting.
related_markets.impact_reason: Confirmation of Warsh as Fed Governor directly impacts his Chair vote.
related_markets.event_title: Who will vote to confirm Kevin Warsh as Fed Chair?

telegram.text: The US appeals court temporarily pauses the trade court ruling against Trump's 10% tariffs under section 122.|FJ

related_markets.title: 1.1T+
related_markets.question: Will the 2026 trade deficit be 1.1T or more?
related_markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026, as reported by the US Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) and the US Census Bureau (USCB) in the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026, expected to be released in February 2027.

Upon publication, the specified release will be made available at: https://www.bea.gov/news/current-releases

The relevant figure may be found in the annual summary under “Exports, Imports, and Balance (exhibit 1)”. Changes in the BEA or USCB’s reporting format will not disqualify a relevant published figure from counting.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026 from the US Bureau of Economic Analysis and the US Census Bureau. If this release is not published by April 30, 2027 ET, another credible source on the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026 will be chosen.

Note: any revisions to the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026 made after the publication of the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026 will not be considered.
related_markets.impact_reason: Court ruling pause on tariffs directly affects trade deficit expectations.

This market will resolve according to the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026, as reported by the US Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) and the US Census Bureau (USCB) in the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026, expected to be released in February 2027.

Upon publication, the specified release will be made available at: https://www.bea.gov/news/current-releases

The relevant figure may be found in the annual summary under “Exports, Imports, and Balance (exhibit 1)”. Changes in the BEA or USCB’s reporting format will not disqualify a relevant published figure from counting.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026 from the US Bureau of Economic Analysis and the US Census Bureau. If this release is not published by April 30, 2027 ET, another credible source on the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026 will be chosen.

Note: any revisions to the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026 made after the publication of the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026 will not be considered.
related_markets.impact_reason: Court ruling pause on tariffs directly affects trade deficit expectations.
